name: Fourby Chili (Black Beans, Beer, and Broccoli)
recipeIngredient: 2 bottles stout (Guinness Extra Or Watney's Cream), or San Miguel Dark beer
2 long (mild) chili peppers, seeded and diced
3 medium Jalapeno chili peppers, seeded and diced
3 serrano chili peppers, seeded and diced
1 cup whole kernel corn (frozen or fresh)
recipeInstructions:
or more, to taste Rinse the black beans, remove any pebbles; then cover with water and let soak overnight. Drain the soaking water, add 1 bottle of stout, and add fresh water to cover the beans plus about 1 inch. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cover partway (it will boil over if fully covered) and cook for 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally. Add water as needed.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add garlic, onions, and all chili peppers. Saute until soft, then add to the beans. Add vinegar and spices and diced tomatoes; simmer for another half hour, stirring. As the liquid from the tomatoes boils off, start adding stout from the second bottle. Reserve about half of this bottle for serving.
Add corn and peanut butter, and simmer for another hour or so, keeping it moist enough (with stout and/or water) so that it doesn't crust over. Stir occasionally.
Best if allowed to sit overnight; reheat for dinner the next day. To serve: cut broccoli florets and steam until crisp (microwave). Serve chili with a sprinkle of chopped onions and grated cheddar cheese on top (vegan omit cheese or use tofu cheese). Broccoli can be stirred in at the last minute, pressed on top for presentation, or placed alongside the chili. Serve with Spanish rice and warmed corn tortillas.
